title: vernonia erigeroides
intro: Vernonia erigeroides, also known as Ironweed, is a flowering plant species native to North America, particularly in the eastern and central regions. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Vernonia erigeroides include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat various skin conditions, such as eczema and dermatitis. Vernonia erigeroides is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine to support the immune system and promote wound healing.
